\section*{Book V Branch Legitimacy --- L4 Generated Shell}
\addcontentsline{toc}{section}{Book V L4 Shell}

\noindent\textit{Book V is the most branch-facing spine book. Its Universal Branch Legitimacy Theorem (UBLT, Thm.~V.8.1) is cited by every branch book in the corpus. The L4 pilot closes the machine-readable dependency gap between the spine and the branch books.}

\subsection*{The Five UBLT Conditions}
\begin{enumerate}
\item Licensed intake: $({\mathcal{C}}, F)$ is a licensed branch candidate
\item Certified source descent: $E = F(\mathbf{U})$ from the universal source
\item Endpoint visibility: $E$ is branch-visible (invariant under source equivalence)
\item Legitimacy witness: a legitimacy witness for $({\mathcal{C}}, F)$ exists
\item No hidden-channel supplementation: no undeclared structure enters $E$
\end{enumerate}

\noindent The UBLT says: lawful branch $\Leftrightarrow$ all five conditions hold.

\subsection*{Branch-Spine Dependency Linkage}
\noindent The following branches cite \texttt{thm:V.UBLT} (Thm.~V.8.1): \texttt{GR}, \texttt{YM}, \texttt{NS}, \texttt{SM}, \texttt{BIO}, \texttt{LING}, \texttt{CRYST}, \texttt{SCC}, \texttt{SPEC}, \texttt{RH}.
With Book~V machine records in place, the governance engine can now trace the full branch-to-spine dependency chain, for example:
\[\texttt{thm:GR.domain} \to \texttt{thm:VII.6.4} \to \texttt{thm:V.UBLT} \to \texttt{def:V.branch-candidate} \to \cdots\]
